title: calophyllum angulare
intro: Calophyllum angulare, also known as the "Aleurites" or "Raintree," is a species of evergreen tree native to the tropical regions of Asia, belonging to the family Calophyllaceae. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its seeds, which contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Calophyllum angulare include the potential treatment of various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin disorders, as well as its use as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. Herbal preparations of Calophyllum angulare include oil and resin extracts, which can be used topically or taken orally in moderation under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
